metadata.title: SYNGO_MF_3006
metadata.comment:
Rationale: Fig.1: "Expression of Abp1 full length and fragments thereof containing the F-actin binding domains induce increased spine length"

Fig.2: "Abp1 overexpression results predominantly in elongation of mushroom-type spines"

Fig.3: "Effects of full-length Abp1 and actin interaction domains of Abp1 on the density of mushroom-shaped spines and synapses"

Fig.4: "Overexpression of Abp1 fragments encompassing the C-terminal SH3 domain highly significantly decreases the density of mushroom spines"

Fig.5: "RNAi-based reduction of Abp1 expression levels reduces the densities of mushroom spines and synapses on hippocampal neurons"

Fig.7: "Abp1 and ProSAP1 or ProSAP2 coexpression leads to highly branched and complex spines with multiple enlarged spine heads"

Discussion: ". Abp1 is not only able to link dynamic actin fibers to ProSAP/Shank proteins and to stabilize actin filaments (Fig. 12A,B), but it is also able to link the SH3 domain interaction partners of the WASP superfamily of Arp2/3 complex activators with F-actin and to activate N-WASP. This leads to Abp1-controlled Arp2/3 complex-mediated nucleation of actin filaments in the microenvironment of the actin- and ProSAP/Shank-rich PSD. Formation of new actin filaments then provides even more Abp1 attachment sides, because Abp1 prefers freshly polymerized actin filaments (Kessels et al., 2000). ". Experimental description: - Overexpressed Dbnl; Abp1; Sh3p7 is of mouse species (traced back to PMID:): ' pGAT2-mAbp1 "
- Neurons are rat: "RNA interference (RNAi) constructs directed against rat Abp1,"
